title: On large subsets of $\mathbb{F}_q^n$ with no three-term arithmetic progression

thm:4.summary: Let $\alpha + \beta + \gamma = 0$ in $\mathbb{F}_q$ with $\gamma \ne 0$, and let $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_q^n$ have no nontrivial solutions $(a_1, a_2, a_3) \in A^3$ to $\alpha a_1 + \beta a_2 + \gamma a_3 = 0$ (other than $a_1 = a_2 = a_3$). Then $|A| \le 3 m_{(q-1)n/3}$, where $m_d$ counts monomials in $n$ variables of total degree at most $d$ with each variable raised to power at most $q-1$. Setting $q = 3$, $\alpha = \beta = \gamma = 1$ recovers the cap-set bound.

cor:5.summary: Cap-set theorem: any $A \subseteq (\mathbb{Z}/3\mathbb{Z})^n$ containing no three-term arithmetic progression has $|A| = o(2.756^n)$. The constant $2.756$ comes from the large-deviations rate $3 e^{-I(2/3)}$ where $I$ is the rate function for the Bernoulli measure on $\{0, 1, 2\}$ at mean $2/3$. Breaks Meshulam's 1995 bound $O(3^n / n)$ exponentially and resolves a longstanding question of Frankl–Graham–Rödl and Brown–Buhler.

prop:2.summary:
Generalized polynomial-method lemma. Let $\alpha +  eta + \gamma = 0$ in $\mathbb{F}_q$, $A \subseteq \mathbb{F}_q^n$, and $P \in S_n^d$ (the space of polynomials of total degree at most $d$ with each variable of degree at most $q-1$). If $P(\alpha a +  eta b) = 0$ for every distinct pair $a, b \in A$, then $P(-\gamma a) 
e 0$ for at most $2 m_{d/2}$ elements $a \in A$. Strengthens Croot–Lev–Pach lem:1 by allowing $\gamma 
e 0$, which is essential for the cap-set application — the $\gamma = 0$ case forces $P(0) = 0$, the $\gamma 
e 0$ case forces $P$ to be small on $-\gamma A$, a much richer constraint.

rem:1.summary: Ellenberg and Gijswijt developed essentially identical arguments independently and simultaneously after Croot–Lev–Pach posted; the paper presents the joint version.

rem:3.summary: prop:2 is essentially the proof of CLP lem:1 with the extra parameter $\gamma$. The CLP form ($\gamma = 0$) gives $P(0) = 0$ once $|A| > 2 m_{d/2}$; the EG form gives the stronger pointwise control on $-\gamma A$, which is what permits the dimension-counting argument in thm:4 to close.

proof:prop:2.summary: Expand $P(\alpha x + \beta y) = \sum_{m,m'} c_{m,m'} m(x) m'(y)$; in each summand at least one factor has degree at most $d/2$, so write $P(\alpha x + \beta y) = \sum_{m \in M_n^{d/2}} m(x) F_m(y) + \sum_{m \in M_n^{d/2}} m(y) G_m(x)$. The $A \times A$ matrix $B_{a,b} = P(\alpha a + \beta b)$ is thus a sum of $2 m_{d/2}$ rank-$1$ matrices, hence has rank at most $2 m_{d/2}$. The hypothesis forces $B$ to be diagonal, so at most $2 m_{d/2}$ diagonal entries are nonzero.

proof:thm:4.summary: Let $V \subseteq S_n^d$ be the space of polynomials vanishing on the complement of $-\gamma A$; $\dim V \ge m_d - q^n + |A|$. By prop:2 applied to any $P \in V$ with maximal support, $|\Sigma_P| \le 2 m_{d/2}$ where $\Sigma_P$ is the support, so $\dim V \le 2 m_{d/2}$. Hence $|A| \le 2 m_{d/2} + (q^n - m_d)$. Using the bijection between $q$-power-free monomials of degree $> d$ and monomials of degree $< (q-1)n - d$, choose $d = 2(q-1)n/3$ to balance both terms, giving $|A| \le 3 m_{(q-1)n/3}$.

proof:cor:5.summary: At $q = 3$, the count $m_{(q-1)n/3} = m_{2n/3}$ is the number of $\{0,1,2\}^n$-vectors with coordinate sum at most $2n/3$. By a large-deviations rate computation, $m_{2n/3} = o(3 e^{-I(2/3)})^n$ where $I$ is the rate function; the optimum gives constant $3 e^{-I(2/3)} < 2.756$. Apply thm:4 with $\alpha = \beta = \gamma = 1$ (so $1 + 1 + 1 = 3 = 0$ in $\mathbb{F}_3$ and $\gamma = 1 \ne 0$).

edges.note: Applies prop:2 to polynomials in the kernel-style space $V$ to bound $\dim V$.
Notes that prop:2 is the CLP polynomial lemma generalized via the $\gamma$ parameter.
Rank decomposition of the $A \times A$ matrix into $2 m_{d/2}$ rank-1 pieces.
Applies prop:2 to the maximal-support element of $V$.

generalized_polynomial_lemma.description: The CLP polynomial-method lemma extended with a free parameter $\gamma$, allowing the conclusion to constrain $P$ on a translate of $A$ rather than just at $0$.

dimension_counting.description: Apply prop:2 to a high-dimensional space of polynomials vanishing on the complement of $-\gamma A$, derive the bound $|A| \le 3 m_{(q-1)n/3}$ via maximal-support and degree-bijection arguments.

cap_set_specialization.description: Specialize to $\mathbb{F}_3^n$ and evaluate the monomial count via large-deviations to extract the constant $2.756$.

attribution.description: Independent and simultaneous discovery; CLP transposition with one extra parameter that makes the cap-set application close.

CLP.title: Progression-free sets in $\mathbb{Z}_4^n$ are exponentially small
